text.title: Django Patterns
text.summary: BaseModel with UUID and timestamps, services layer for business logic, thin views, serializers, and admin with fieldsets.

rule.statement: Business logic lives in services.py, not views. Services receive domain types (email: str, user: User), not request objects. Wrap multi-step DB operations in @transaction.atomic.
rule.rationale: Thin views delegate to services. Services are callable from Celery tasks, management commands, and tests without an HTTP request context.

example.code:
from rest_framework.views import APIView from rest_framework.response import Response from rest_framework import status from . import services from .serializers import CreateUserRequest, UserResponse
class UserCreateView(APIView): def post(self, request): serializer = CreateUserRequest(data=request.data) serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True) user = services.create_user(**serializer.validated_data) return Response(UserResponse(user).data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
